perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the other party, neither it nor its Representatives will disclose to any other person (other than to such party’s Representatives who agree, or are otherwise bound, not to disclose the following except as contemplated by this Agreement) any of the other party’s Evaluation Material, the fact that discussions between the parties are taking place concerning the Transaction, or any terms or other facts relating to the Transaction. Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, the Evaluation Material shall only be used by a party and its Representatives to evaluate, negotiate, facilitate or pursue a Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the other party, neither it nor its Representatives will disclose to any other person any of the other party’s Evaluation Material, the fact that discussions between the parties are taking place concerning the Transaction, or any terms or other facts relating to the Transaction. Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, the Evaluation Material shall only be used by a party and its Representatives to evaluate, negotiate, facilitate or pursue a Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause '(other than to such party’s Representatives who agree, or are otherwise bound, not to disclose the following except as contemplated by this Agreement)' it implies that the party's Representatives are also unable to see the Evaluation Material. It creates a contradiction, because later in the text, the Evaluation Material can be used by a party and its Representatives to evaluate, negotiate, facilitate or pursue a Transaction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the other party, neither it nor its Representatives will disclose to any other person any of the other party’s Evaluation Material, the fact that discussions between the parties are taking place concerning the Transaction, or any terms or other facts relating to the Transaction. Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, the Evaluation Material shall only be used by a party and its Representatives to evaluate, negotiate, facilitate or pursue a Transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows Representatives of a party to view Evaluation Material if they agree or are bound not to disclose it. The changed text removes this exception, implying that Representatives cannot view the Evaluation Material at all. The contradicted text states that 'Evaluation Material shall only be used by a party and its Representatives to evaluate, negotiate, facilitate or pursue a Transaction.' This directly contradicts the changed text because the Representatives would now be unable to use the Evaluation Material, creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon written request from the other party, each party shall return or destroy (at the receiving party’s option) all copies of the Evaluation Material (including all documents based thereon) in its or its Representatives’ possession, provided, that the receiving party shall only be required to use commercially reasonable efforts to return or destroy any Evaluation Material created pursuant to its or its Representatives’ standard electronic backup and archival procedures. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the receiving party and each of its Representatives may each retain one copy of the Evaluation Material to show compliance with this Agreement or to comply with applicable law, rule or regulation (or, in the case of a party’s advisors, professional standards).
perturbation.changed_text: Promptly upon written request from the other party, each party shall return or destroy (at the receiving party’s option) all copies of the Evaluation Material (including all documents based thereon) in its or its Representatives’ possession, provided, that the receiving party shall only be required to use commercially reasonable efforts to return or destroy any Evaluation Material created pursuant to its or its Representatives’ standard electronic backup and archival procedures. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the receiving party may each retain one copy of the Evaluation Material to show compliance with this Agreement or to comply with applicable law, rule or regulation.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause '(or, in the case of a party’s advisors, professional standards)' from the final sentence of paragraph 7, creates an in-text contradiction by implying that a party's advisors can not retain one copy of the Evaluation Material to show compliance. With the removal, if a party's advisors need to show compliance, due to professional standards, but do not have a copy they can not, it is contradictory to the statement 'the receiving party may each retain one copy of the Evaluation Material to show compliance with this Agreement or to comply with applicable law, rule or regulation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Promptly upon written request from the other party, each party shall return or destroy (at the receiving party’s option) all copies of the Evaluation Material (including all documents based thereon) in its or its Representatives’ possession, provided, that the receiving party shall only be required to use commercially reasonable efforts to return or destroy any Evaluation Material created pursuant to its or its Representatives’ standard electronic backup and archival procedures. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the receiving party may each retain one copy of the Evaluation Material to show compliance with this Agreement or to comply with applicable law, rule or regulation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the clause '(or, in the case of a party’s advisors, professional standards)' from the original text. The explanation states that this creates a contradiction because it implies that a party's advisors cannot retain a copy of the Evaluation Material to show compliance with professional standards, even if those standards require it. The contradicted text which is the same as the original, allows the receiving party to retain one copy of the Evaluation Material to show compliance with applicable law, rule or regulation. The omission of the clause therefore creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'by any of its Representatives' from section 10, it creates an in-text contradiction. Without the removed clause, there is no explicit statement for who's actions a party is responsible for, which is the original contract refers to its Representatives. Section 2 states 'Each party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the other party, neither it nor its Representatives will disclose'. By removing the Representatives statement from Section 10, and only talking about the 'party', there is a in-text contradiction, since the Representatives will disclose, but the 'party' is the only one responsible
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the other party, neither it nor its Representatives will disclose to any other person (other than to such party’s Representatives who agree, or are otherwise bound, not to disclose the following except as contemplated by this Agreement) any of the other party’s Evaluation Material, the fact that discussions between the parties are taking place concerning the Transaction, or any terms or other facts relating to the Transaction. Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, the Evaluation Material shall only be used by a party and its Representatives to evaluate, negotiate, facilitate or pursue a Transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original text makes each party responsible for a breach by its Representatives. The changed text removes 'by any of its Representatives', making a party responsible for any breach, regardless of who caused it. The contradicted text states that 'neither it nor its Representatives will disclose', implying the Representatives have separate obligations. The removal of 'Representatives' in the changed text creates a contradiction, because the party is the only one responsible for the breach according to the changed section, while the Representatives have separate obligations based on the contradicted text. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All matters arising out of, or relating to this Agreement shall be governed by the procedural and substantive laws of the State of Delaware without regard to any conflicts of law principles.
perturbation.changed_text: All matters arising out of, or relating to this Agreement shall be governed by the procedural laws of the State of Delaware without regard to any conflicts of law principles.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and substantive' from Section 11, it creates an in-text contradiction. With only being governed by procedural laws, any claims would not have 'substantive' laws as well. However section 8 states 'Unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding the Transaction has been executed by the parties, no agreement with respect to the Transaction shall be deemed to exist between the parties and neither party will be under any obligation whatsoever with respect to the Transaction by virtue of this Agreement or any oral expression except for the matters specifically agreed to herein.' The matters specifically agreed to herein, relate to substantive agreements made in this Agreement, however with substantive laws removed from Section 11, the matters specifically agreed to herein, are not governed by any law.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding the Transaction has been executed by the parties, no agreement with respect to the Transaction shall be deemed to exist between the parties and neither party will be under any obligation whatsoever with respect to the Transaction by virtue of this Agreement or any oral expression except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. The term “definitive agreement” does not include a term sheet or any other preliminary written agreement. Each party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to provide or not provide Evaluation Material under this Agreement, to reject any and all proposals and to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'and substantive' from the governing law clause, specifying that only procedural laws of Delaware govern the agreement. The contradicted text states that obligations exist 'except for the matters specifically agreed to herein.' The explanation argues that because the matters specifically agreed to relate to substantive agreements, removing substantive law from the governing law clause creates a contradiction, because those matters are no longer governed by law. This introduces a direct contradiction: the agreement contemplates substantive obligations (in the contradicted text), but the changed text removes substantive law as the governing law. Therefore, a clear and direct conflict exists, leading to potential unenforceability.
